key_themes: Insider buying demonstrating confidence post-equity offering
Operational success with strong SS-6H well flow rates
Significant capital raise (~$100M+) to fund Beetaloo Basin development
Bullish analyst upgrades with price targets up to $74
Strategic importance for Australian energy security and net-zero transition
sources: 12+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, TipRanks, Business Wire, CNBC)
15+ recent X posts (primarily insider buying and operations)
ASX forums (HotCopper) on director notices and announcements
summary: Tamboran Resources (NYSE: TBN), focused on natural gas development in Australia's Beetaloo Basin, shows positive sentiment after completing a public offering at $35/share that strengthens its balance sheet for growth.[[1]](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TBN)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TBN/) Multiple directors and affiliated trusts have purchased shares (including a ~$370K buy by Director Phillip Pace), signaling conviction despite dilution from the raise.[[3]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TBN/form-4-tamboran-resources-corp-insider-trading-activity-d0d88a2d183e.html)[[4]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/tamboran-resources-nysetbn-director-phillip-pace-acquires-10000-shares-2026-04-15/) Recent strong IP20 flow rates (11.9 MMcf/d from SS-6H), new farm-in partnerships, analyst Buy ratings, and price target increases (e.g., Piper Sandler to $74) underpin optimism, echoed in investor posts highlighting insiders “loading up” and the company’s energy transition role.[[5]](https://www.cnn.com/markets/stocks/TBN)[[6]](https://x.com/CEOStockWatcher/status/2044822405353181397)
